prompt: Photoreal cinematic pattern interrupt macro shot in a modern bathroom, vertical 9:16. Camera is INSIDE the mirrored medicine cabinet looking outward. A hand yanks the cabinet door open abruptly and an absurd overload of generic, unbranded hair-loss products bursts out like a pressure release: dozens of plain shampoo bottles, dropper bottles with clear liquid, pill bottles, supplement jars, scalp oil bottles, a derma roller, foam canisters. The objects fly straight toward the lens, narrowly missing it, clattering loudly, bouncing off the sink and counter. Add satisfying micro-details: liquid droplets, powder dust, condensation, tiny plastic scuffs, high-speed slow-motion moments mixed with real-time bursts (fast-open -> slow-mo avalanche -> fast final clatter). Bright clean bathroom lighting, crisp reflections, shallow depth of field, handheld micro-shake for realism. Keep the person’s face out of frame. End on the counter completely covered in products, cabinet empty, one bottle spinning to a stop near the camera. No captions, no on-screen text, no logos, no readable labels, no brands. Ambient bathroom SFX only (cabinet snap open, plastic clacks, bottles sliding), no music, no speech, no dialogue.
